description: This weak-beam transmission electron micrograph (imaging condition near g:3–4g, zone axis near [0 0 1], g vector 2 0 0 as arrowed) displays the dislocation substructure in room-temperature deformed Fe–28% Al, the alloy composition at the peak of the work-hardening curve. The main field of view together with two upper insets magnifies superdislocation reactions labeled “r,” showing straight segments produced by interacting glissile superdislocations. Caption and accompanying text state that these reactions generate sessile edge <100> dislocation locks via the interaction of two superdislocations with different <111> Burgers vectors on distinct {1 1 0} glide planes—exemplified by [1 1 1] + [1 1¯ 1] forming a [1 0 0] edge pair along [0 1 0] lying on a (0 0 1) plane. The 0.2 μm scale bar underscores the nanoscale defect distribution. These immobile locks obstruct further superdislocation glide, furnishing direct microstructural evidence that dislocation locking underlies the sharp work-hardening maximum near 28% Al.
